state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Montana’s northern tier is a pulse-crop innovator while eastern ranges support spring wheat and cattle drives.
fun_facts: Montana rotations now include large lentil and chickpea acres for export.
Huckleberries remain a prized late-summer forage crop.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Wyoming agriculture is overwhelmingly extensive grazing with irrigated beet slices along major rivers.
fun_facts: Wyoming ships more beef on the hoof than almost any state per capita.
High-altitude lettuce and root crops finish sweetest before first hard frost.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Colorado blends Rocky Mountain ranching with specialty fruit on the Western Slope and rapid indoor ag near Denver.
fun_facts: Palisade peaches owe sweetness to hot days and cool nights.
Craft malt from Colorado barley loops breweries back to farm fields.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: New Mexico’s Hatch chile harvest is a sensory season—pecan orchards follow Rio Grande water rights.
fun_facts: Hatch Valley chiles receive a protected reputation like wine appellations.
Navajo-Churro sheep tie heritage fiber to high-desert grazing.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Utah’s mountain valleys produce tart cherries and apples while desert ranches supply interstate beef.
fun_facts: Brigham Young urged early settlers to plant fruit trees—orchards still ring many towns.
Great Salt Lake wetlands influence microclimates for specialty melons.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Arizona’s vegetable belt flips the calendar—Yuma ships leafy greens while upland pecans mature in fall.
fun_facts: Yuma County often plants the nation’s first winter lettuce after the Salinas gap.
Date palms in Bard produce a distinctly Southwestern harvest.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Nevada irrigates desert valleys for alfalfa and exported hay—urban edges host hydroponic berries.
fun_facts: Vegas resorts once drove unusual local greenhouse herb demand.
Great Basin drought sharply limits dryland cropping.
